title: Usage of multiline --build-arg not works (use only first line) - docker build works
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run podman build with --build-arg
`podman build --format docker --build-arg SSH_PRIVATE_KEY="${SSH_PRIVATE_KEY}"`
SSH_PRIVATE_KEY env variable contains a multiline SSH key

2. Print passed build-arg to console during a Docker build

**Describe the results you received:**

Check output - output will be just: `-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Full SSH key 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
$ podman version
Version:            1.1.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
Built:              Tue Mar  5 18:10:31 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
$ buildah version
Version:         1.7
Go Version:      go1.11.5
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Found on Azure k8s Gitlab CI runners.

comments.body:
@jakubjosef could you give me an example of how you set up the SSH_PRIVATE_KEY envvar please?

Just include in the example the first and last lines of a private key

-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

and leave all the good stuff out.  My gut is this is some kind of shell/cli handling issue.

We just recently fixed an almost identical issue for Podman run, and it was
an issue with the type of flag we used - string slice vs string array.
Can't remember which was correct unfortunately.

On Thu, Apr 4, 2019, 08:16 Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @jakubjosef <https://github.com/jakubjosef> could you give me an example
> of how you set up the SSH_PRIVATE_KEY envvar please?
>
> Just include in the example the first and last lines of a private key
>
> -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
> -----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
>
> and leave all the good stuff out. My gut is this is some kind of shell/cli
> handling issue.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2846#issuecomment-479872974>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCCHKar528cxNXOKlUvxeaBGc0j8qks5vde0GgaJpZM4ccUAq>
> .
>
@mheon, I thought I remembered seeing a similar issue recently, but didn't find it on a quick scan, will look again, thx!
Based on https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1467, I'm thinking StringArray works better for those.

Can someone merge that PR.  And then we can close this one also.
Then @jakubjosef can verify it it fixes his problem.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Actually this env variable is set by Gitlab CI.
If I print this variable I just receive multiline string output. I don't know if I use string slice or string array,  how can I check it? @mheon?

@jakubjosef I'm pretty sure containers/buildah#1467 by @rhatdan will cure this issue for you once we vendor Buildah into Podman with that fix in place.  The string slice come from the Buildah option handling code, so nothing on your side or GitLab for that.  Hopefully we can get you something to test by early next week at the latest.
@TomSweeneyRedHat You you open a Vendor buildah pr?
I could, or as discussed in another PR, perhaps we should make Buildah 1.7.3 first?
I believe this works, Reopen if you disagree.
